decks.notes: This Temur Control deck focuses on using a variety of spells and creature abilities to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Utilizing cards like Burst Lightning, Spell Snare, and Stock Up, the deck aims to keep the opponent's board in check while maintaining card advantage through card draw effects like Consult the Star Charts and Analyze the Pollen. By managing the battlefield and outlasting the opponent, the deck can set up powerful plays with Mightform Harmonizer and other creatures to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to focus on control and utilizing a mix of powerful creatures and spells to dominate the game. A combination of removal spells like Lightning Helix and Inevitable Defeat, along with powerful creatures like Aang, Swift Savior and Ashling, Rekindled, allows the player to control the board and disrupt their opponent's plans. The deck also includes various lands for mana fixing and consistency, as well as cards like Get Lost and Stock Up for additional board control and card advantage. Ultimately, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory through carefully planned strategies and powerful plays.

The main goal of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent quickly with a fast and aggressive playstyle. By utilizing creatures with abilities that allow for efficient mana ramping and monster summoning, you can establish a strong board presence early on. Earthbender Ascension and Sapling Nursery further enhance your creature production, while cards like Mossborn Hydra and Mightform Harmonizer provide additional power and versatility. Utilize your mana wisely and maintain pressure on your opponent to secure a swift victory.

This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its fast-paced creature plays and powerful aggression. By utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub and Ouroboroid to establish an early board presence, as well as utilizing cards like Nova Hellkite and Overlord of the Boilerbilges to maintain pressure on opponents. The inclusion of cards like Nature's Rhythm and Multiversal Passage allows for mana ramping and fixing, ensuring a smooth curve of plays. Overall, this deck prioritizes speed and efficiency in dealing damage to opponents, leading to swift victories in 1 vs 1 matchups.

This deck focuses on utilizing the Bant Airbending archetype to quickly assemble powerful combos with cards like Aang, at the Crossroads and Aang, Swift Savior. By utilizing creatures like Appa, Steadfast Guardian and Badgermole Cub, along with spells like Interdimensional Web Watch and Airbender Ascension, the deck aims to control the battlefield and overwhelm opponents with a swift and devastating offense. The deck also includes mana ramp with Llanowar Elves and fixing with lands like Breeding Pool and Hallowed Fountain to ensure consistency in executing its combos.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm and quickly defeat the opponent by playing aggressive red creatures and utilizing direct damage spells to clear the way for attacks. With a low mana curve and efficient removal options, this deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent and finish the game swiftly. The creatures are chosen for their speed and power, while the spells provide additional reach and control. Overall, the strategy is to be proactive and relentless in dealing damage to secure victory before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively deal damage to the opponent as quickly as possible using a combination of powerful creatures and direct damage spells. The deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent by constantly attacking with efficient red creatures like Burnout Bashtronaut and Nova Hellkite, while also using burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike to finish off opponents or deal with threats. The inclusion of cards like Hexing Squelcher and Sunspine Lynx helps to control the board and maintain the pressure.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of powerful creatures and disruptive spells. The key strategy involves playing aggressive creatures like Ashling, Rekindled and Flamebraider, while also using spells like Deceit and Wistfulness to control the pace of the game. The deck also includes cards like Roaming Throne and Vibrance to maintain board presence and apply pressure. With a mix of card advantage and efficient removal, this deck aims to quickly build a strong board state and outpace the opponent to secure a victory.

The main focus of this Izzet Control deck is to manipulate the board state and disrupt the opponent's plans through a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw. By utilizing a mix of instants and sorceries, the deck aims to control the pace of the game, create card advantage, and eventually overpower the opponent with efficient spells and powerful creatures. The mana base is designed to support a steady stream of blue and red spells, allowing the player to consistently cast their powerful control cards and maintain control of the battlefield.

This deck focuses on controlling the board with spells and abilities that deal damage, draw cards, and manipulate opponent's resources. By combining Izzet Control cards with powerful abilities such as Firebending Lesson and Stormchaser's Talent, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents. Key cards include Gran-Gran for board presence, Monument to Endurance for card advantage, and Abandon Attachments for disruption. The deck's mana base allows for consistent casting of spells and abilities, ensuring that the player can maintain control over the game until victory is achieved.

The main focus of this deck is to control the opponent's board with various spells and abilities, while also utilizing the Izzet colors of blue and red to deal damage and increase card advantage. The deck includes a variety of spells like Abandon Attachments and Combustion Technique to remove threats, as well as card draw spells like Accumulate Wisdom and Stormchaser's Talent to maintain resources. Monument to Endurance and Artist's Talent provide additional utility and potentially game-changing effects. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure efficient access to both colors of mana.

This deck focuses on utilizing control elements such as removal, card draw, and counter spells to manage the opponent's threats and establish a strong board presence. The key strategy involves maximizing the value of spells with instant speed interaction and tempo plays, while using powerful creatures like Gran-Gran to pressure the opponent. Monument to Endurance helps to generate card advantage and maintain resources, while cards like Abandon Attachments and Firebending Lesson provide efficient removal options. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent through resource management and strategic decision-making.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By using cards like Gran-Gran and Monument to Endurance, the deck aims to outvalue opponents and maintain board control. Cards like Abandon Attachments and Combustion Technique can help deal with threats on the board, while Accumulate Wisdom and Firebending Lesson provide card advantage. The deck also includes some versatile spells like It'll Quench Ya! and Boomerang Basics for additional control options. Overall, the strategy revolves around efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent quickly with aggressive creatures and spells. By utilizing cards like Elusive Otter and Slickshot Show-Off, you can apply pressure early on and keep up the tempo with cards like Burst Lightning and Opt. Additionally, cards like Secret Identity and Stormchaser's Talent provide additional utility and card advantage to help maintain your aggression. The mana base is constructed to ensure consistent access to all required colors, allowing for smooth gameplay and maximum efficiency in executing your game plan.

The main concept of this Temur Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a blend of aggressive creatures and spells. This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with cards like Ashling, Rekindled and Roaming Throne, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Deceit and Winternight Stories. The deck aims to pressure the opponent early in the game and maintain that pressure throughout, utilizing a mix of creature-based threats and spell-based removal to control the battlefield and secure a victory.

The deck focuses on maintaining control over the board by utilizing a mix of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw. The main strategy is to control the game by disrupting opponents' plays with counterspells like Essence Scatter and Spell Snare, while also dealing with threats using Burst Lightning and Sear. Card advantage is generated through spells like Consult the Star Charts and Stock Up, allowing the player to outvalue their opponent and ultimately come out on top. The deck also incorporates some versatile creatures like Mightform Harmonizer and Icetill Explorer to apply pressure while still maintaining control.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent and secure victory. With a mix of flying creatures, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers like Ajani and Elspeth, the deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain control of the board throughout the game. By combining white and blue cards in the Azorius colors, the deck is able to have a versatile range of threats and answers to handle various board states and opponent strategies. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure consistent access to all necessary colors and resources.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing a mix of creatures and spells to overwhelm the opponent and control the board. Aang and Aven Interrupter provide quick and efficient threats, while Floodpits Drowner and Voice of Victory add additional pressure. Enduring Curiosity and Enduring Innocence provide card advantage, while Get Lost and No More Lies disrupt the opponent's strategy. High Noon and Seam Rip offer removal options, with Spell Snare countering key spells. Ajani, Outland Chaperone offers additional support for the aggressive strategy. The deck's mana base includes Floodfarm Verge, Hallowed Fountain, and other dual lands to ensure consistency in casting spells.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a combination of creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It uses a mix of evasion, disruption, and card draw to maintain pressure and control the game tempo. The creatures are low-cost and efficient, enabling fast starts and early aggression, while the spells provide additional reach and removal options. The mana base is designed to support both the aggressive game plan and the deck's color requirements. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining card advantage.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of control spells and card draw to outlast opponents. It synergizes cards that remove threats and maintain card advantage, allowing for strategic decision-making and resource management. The main win condition involves overwhelming the opponent with efficient removal spells and eventually closing out the game with powerful combo plays. Key cards such as Gran-Gran, Combustion Technique, and Firebending Lesson help control the board and set up for a decisive victory. Overall, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and dictate the flow of play.

The main idea behind this deck is to control the game by using removal spells and countering opponent's threats, while also actively drawing cards to maintain a steady flow of resources. The deck focuses on using a combination of burn spells and card draw to establish dominance on the battlefield, while also incorporating some control elements like counterspells. By utilizing a mix of removal, card draw, and control spells, this Izzet Control deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game using a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card advantage to outlast opponents. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage through drawing and filtering cards. By utilizing a mix of instant-speed interaction and powerful spells, the deck aims to establish dominance on the battlefield and close out the game with powerful finishers. The inclusion of unique cards like Gran-Gran and Monument to Endurance adds an extra layer of versatility and power to the deck's strategy.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana using Llanowar Elves and Sapling Nursery to play powerful creatures like Mossborn Hydra and Fecund Greenshell. It also utilizes Earthbender Ascension to boost its creatures and Archdruid's Charm for removal. The inclusion of Escape Tunnel and Promising Vein ensures protection and additional mana sources. This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of aggressive creatures early in the game and maintain dominance throughout with efficient mana usage and strong synergy between the cards.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly flood the board with creatures and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. Utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub, Llanowar Elves, and Sazh's Chocobo to ramp up mana and maintain board presence, while also utilizing Earthbender Ascension and Sapling Nursery to buff and protect creatures. The objective is to maintain a strong board presence and pressure the opponent with efficient creature plays, ultimately leading to a quick victory through early aggression and relentless pressure.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to deal damage as quickly as possible, utilizing efficient creatures like Burnout Bashtronaut and Hexing Squelcher along with direct damage spells like Burst Lightning and Shock. By maintaining a fast-paced and aggressive playstyle, the goal is to reduce the opponent's life total to zero before they have a chance to stabilize or mount a strong defense. The deck is built to prioritize speed and damage output over long-term value or defense.

This deck focuses on quickly building a strong board presence using efficient low-cost creatures and ramping with cards like Llanowar Elves and Earthbender Ascension. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of aggressive creatures like Badgermole Cub and Mossborn Hydra, while also having some control elements with cards like Winternight Stories and Escape Tunnel. The deck combines the speed of aggro decks with the versatility of Simic colors to create a well-rounded aggressive strategy that can adapt to different opponents and situations.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly establish board presence and apply pressure on the opponent through aggressive creatures and efficient mana ramp. By using cards like Llanowar Elves and Nature's Rhythm, the deck aims to accelerate its game plan and overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures like Badgermole Cub and Quantum Riddler. The inclusion of utility cards like Wistfulness and Mockingbird provide additional value and flexibility. The deck also has a couple of powerful finishers in Craterhoof Behemoth and Explosive Prodigy to secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing various cards to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bring them back to the battlefield for explosive combos. By using cards like Deceit and Requiting Hex to manipulate the graveyard, along with powerful reanimation spells like Overlord of the Balemurk and Emet-Selch, Unsundered, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a sudden assault of massive creatures. With the ability to quickly recover from disruption thanks to cards like Multiversal Passage and Preacher of the Schism, this deck is designed to be a fast and aggressive reanimator combo deck.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful reanimation spells to bring back key creatures from the graveyard, such as Bringer of the Last Gift and Overlord of the Balemurk, to create powerful combos and overwhelm the opponent. It also includes cards like Deceit and Wistfulness to manipulate the graveyard and set up for explosive plays. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the deck's various color requirements, allowing for smooth casting of spells and ensuring the deck's consistency in executing its game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to slow down the opponent's plays while generating value and card advantage through controlling spells and creatures. The deck focuses on using a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw to outlast and outvalue the opponent. Additionally, the deck utilizes a mix of creatures with abilities that help control the board and generate advantage. The mana base is designed to ensure consistency in casting spells and provide options for varying situations. Ultimately, the goal is to control the game and overpower the opponent with powerful late-game threats.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw to outvalue and outlast the opponent. By using a mix of creatures and spells to control the battlefield, the deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a strong advantage, ultimately leading to victory. Additionally, the deck includes a mana base with a mix of different colors to ensure it has access to a variety of spells and resources.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to control the game through a combination of countering spells, bouncing opponent's permanents, and drawing cards. By utilizing cards like Valley Floodcaller, Consult the Star Charts, and Roiling Dragonstorm, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up its own resources. The inclusion of cards like Get Out and Sleight of Hand allows for more flexibility and consistency in accessing key pieces of the deck. With a mix of removal, card draw, and disruption, this deck aims to outlast opponents and secure victory through strong control play.
